perturbation.original_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; furthermore, each party is fully responsible for the accuracy of the information provided by all other parties, regardless of knowledge or belief.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that each party is only responsible for their own information, unless they know or should know of inaccuracies in other parties' information. The modified text contradicts this by stating that each party is fully responsible for the accuracy of all information, regardless of their knowledge. This creates uncertainty regarding the level of due diligence required by each party and shifts the risk profile significantly. This is an In-Text contradiction because we are modifying what was previously stated in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; furthermore, each party is fully responsible for the accuracy of the information provided by all other parties, regardless of knowledge or belief. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited each party's responsibility to their own information unless they had knowledge of inaccuracies in others' information. The changed text imposes full responsibility on each party for the accuracy of all information, regardless of knowledge, which directly contradicts the limited responsibility outlined previously in the same section. This is a direct and significant change in obligation, making the two statements incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until terminated by unanimous consent of all parties; however, any individual party may unilaterally withdraw from specific obligations under this agreement by providing written notice, but the core agreement remains binding.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the agreement remains in full force until revoked by a party, but the revocation only applies to the revoking party. The modified text introduces a condition of 'unanimous consent' for termination, directly contradicting the initial revocation clause. It further complicates matters by allowing unilateral withdrawal from 'specific obligations,' while keeping the 'core agreement' binding, creating ambiguity about which parts of the agreement can be unilaterally withdrawn from. This directly creates in-text contradiction because the first sentence requires unanimous content from the parties involved, but the following sentence expresses that there can be an unilateral withdrawal from the agreement, even when its stated before, that the agreement is still binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until terminated by unanimous consent of all parties; however, any individual party may unilaterally withdraw from specific obligations under this agreement by providing written notice, but the core agreement remains binding. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Agreement this 14t h day of February 2020.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a clear contradiction. Originally, any party could revoke the agreement with respect to themselves. The change requires unanimous consent for termination while also allowing individual parties to withdraw from 'specific obligations' but the core agreement remains binding. This contradicts the unanimous consent requirement for full termination, creating a situation where a party can withdraw from obligations while still being bound by a 'core agreement' that is ill-defined. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, but it still creates a contradiction. The first sentence states unanimous consent from all parties, but the second sentence states that an unilateral withdraw from the agreement can occur, even when the parties are considered to be in the agreement. This directly violates the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of duplicates, but each duplicate must be identical and considered a separate agreement, requiring individual interpretation and enforcement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text indicates that multiple counterparts constitute *one* instrument. The modified version states that each 'duplicate' (counterpart) is a *separate* agreement, requiring individual interpretation and enforcement. This is a direct contradiction, creating ambiguity about the intended relationship between the duplicates. Each duplicate is considered a separate agreement, requiring individual interpretation and enforcement is an in-text contradiction of what was previously stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of duplicates, but each duplicate must be identical and considered a separate agreement, requiring individual interpretation and enforcement. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Agreement this 14t h day of February 2020. CLARUS IV-A, L.P. By: Clarus IV GP, L.P., its general partner By: Blackstone Clarus GP L.P., its general partner By: Blackstone Clarus GP L.L.C., its general partner By: /s/ John G. Finley Name: John G. Finley Title: Chief Legal Officer
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the nature of 'counterparts' to 'duplicates,' specifying that each duplicate is a separate agreement. The contradicted text repeats the changed text indicating that each duplicate is a separate agreement and should be considered independently. The original text stated that they constituted one and the same instrument. Therefore, the change has introduced an in-text contradiction, as multiple duplicates are now considered separate agreements, necessitating individual interpretation and enforcement, conflicting with the original intent of them forming a single instrument. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', where different terms are used leading to conflicting obligations.
